title: Write an event-time windowed aggregation
description: Steps to assign timestamps and watermarks and emit a correct tumbling aggregate tolerant of bounded lateness.
body: Use when implementing a time-based aggregate over a stream. Do not key the window on ingestion time — late events then land in the wrong window. Actions: assign the event timestamp, set a watermark covering measured lateness, define the tumbling window with allowed lateness and a side output, and verify a replay reproduces the live aggregate. Validation signals include each event landing in the correct event-time window and identical aggregates between live and replayed runs. Risk boundary: a too-tight watermark drops valid late events; if the dropped-late metric is non-trivial, treat the window as lossy and escalate to widen the bound or fix the source before relying on the output.
